commit.message:
Prioritize viewport items in summary fetch queue on scroll (#73)

When load() queues all articles in FIFO order, fast scrolling on mobile
left viewport items stuck behind hundreds of earlier items. Now request()
moves already-pending items to the front of the queue and inserts new
items at the front, so the enrich scheduler's viewport-aware calls get
fetched first.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix polyphase channelizer branch decomposition (type-1 → type-2)

The polyphase filterbank used a type-1 decomposition (E_k[n] = h[nM+k])
but the analysis filterbank with DFT requires type-2 (E_k[n] = h[nM+M-1-k]).
This caused off-center tones to map to wrong channels — e.g. a +2000 Hz
tone landed in Ch 0 instead of Ch 1. Tones at exact channel centers
worked by luck due to DFT orthogonality, masking the bug.

Fix: reverse the column order during prototype decomposition. All tones
now map to the correct nearest channel with 36 dB isolation. Added
parametrized tests for off-center tone mapping and 3-tone detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Super Saiyan God upgrade: bf16 golden chain, micro-training UI, pipeline hardening

Phase 1 — Critical Pipeline Fixes:
- bf16 golden chain in safe_merge.py: HF bf16 weights are now source of truth
  for all merges. GGUF derived via convert_hf_to_gguf + llama-quantize.
  Zero quantization drift across unlimited merge cycles.
- Auto adapter_config.json normalization in train_v5.py: fixes absolute HF
  cache paths to canonical "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-14B-Instruct" after save.
- Removed --attn-only from consolidation_train.py: consolidation now uses
  all 7 LoRA modules (MLP adds 5-15% quality improvement).
- Auto GGUF conversion post-training: train_v5.py auto-runs
  convert_lora_to_gguf.py to produce adapter.gguf alongside PEFT files.

Phase 2 — Micro-Training Flywheel:
- New scripts/batch_splitter.py: splits large JSONL into 500-pair batches
  with shuffle, manifest tracking, and auto metadata stripping.
- New scripts/preflight_check.py: automated pre-flight validator with 7
  checks (HF offline, metadata types, CRLF, disk space, GPU VRAM, base
  model, HF cache). Integrated into run_full_cycle.sh.
- New API endpoints in app.py:
  - POST /api/lora/micro-train — launch training in WSL tmux session
  - GET /api/lora/training-status — poll step/loss/stage/log from WSL
  - POST /api/lora/stop-training — kill training tmux session
  - POST /api/lora/prepare-batches — split JSONL via batch_splitter
  - GET /api/eval/ledger — return score_ledger.json for visualization

Phase 3 — Training UI Upgrades:
- System health bar in base.html: all pages show LLM/Embedding/GPU/Training
  status indicators, auto-polls every 30s.
- Training pipeline monitor in forge.html: live 7-step pipeline stage
  indicator, progress bar, loss display, collapsible log tail, stop button.
- One-click micro-train launcher in forge.html: domain selector, data path,
  version fields, launches run_full_cycle.sh via WSL tmux.
- Graph management buttons in graph_explorer.html: Rebuild Graph + Re-embed.
- Score All Books button in dashboard.html.
- Score ledger visualization in eval.html: domain progression table showing
  all versions with color-coded scores.

Phase 4 — Pipeline Hardening:
- Rich checkpoint state in run_full_cycle.sh: saves version, domain, data
  path, timestamp (not just step number) for better resume.
- Auto disk cleanup after promotion: keeps 3 GGUF versions + 2 bf16
  checkpoints, clears HF dataset cache, reports free space.
- Preflight integration: run_full_cycle.sh auto-runs preflight_check.py.
- Updated CLAUDE.md with v1.2 pipeline docs, training UI docs, micro-
  training flywheel instructions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
NT glossing wave 2: fix 15+ wrong meanings, strip artifacts

Critical word fixes:
- leoleo: guard (was shepherd); leoleo mamoe = shepherd
- tuugamau: tomb (was lampstand)
- talitane: harlot (was shield)
- moa: rooster (was banana blossom)
- fasia: killed (was struck)
- aue: woe (was dictionary-style gloss)
- maʻi: sick, gasegase: disease (new)
- faʻataʻitaiga: parable (new)

Structural fixes:
- Remove generic "le au" = "the group of" (127 hits)
- Add 25+ specific "le au [noun]" phrases (brethren, saints, etc.)
- Post-processing: "the I" → "the", "from he" → "from him"
- Strip leading spaces from all glosses (241 hits)
- SW bumped to v48

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(spaces): auto-detect and expand Matrix spaces in reconciliation (#73)

* docs(spaces): add space support design doc

Defines auto-detection of Matrix spaces in GROUP_MAPPINGS,
child room expansion during reconciliation, and symmetric
join/kick ordering for spaces and their children.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(spaces): add implementation plan

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: add .worktrees/ to gitignore

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(synapse): add get_space_children to MatrixService trait

Define the method on the trait, implement it in SynapseClient using the
room state admin endpoint (filtering m.space.child events with non-empty
content), and stub it in all mock implementations so the project compiles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(reconcile): expand space mappings to include child rooms

Wire MatrixService (replacing RoomManagementApi) into reconcile_membership
and preview_membership. Add expand_targets helper that discovers space
children via get_space_children and includes them in reconciliation.

Joins proceed in forward order (space first, then children); kicks proceed
in reverse (children first, then space). Space child discovery failures
fall back to treating the room as a single target with a warning.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(reconcile): add space expansion edge case tests

Cover reverse kick ordering through spaces, mixed space/room mappings,
and graceful fallback when space child discovery fails.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(preview): add space children expansion test

Verify that preview_membership expands space mappings into child rooms,
confirming the space ID plus all children appear in the joins list.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(lifecycle): expand spaces in kick_from_all_mapped_rooms

Discover space children before kicking and iterate targets
in reverse order (children first, then space). Update the
test mock to track kick order via kicked_rooms and add a
test verifying the correct ordering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: remove dead RoomManagementApi trait

Reconcile handlers now use MatrixService via state.synapse
directly. The RoomManagementApi trait, its impl on SynapseClient,
the AppState.room_mgmt field, and the MockSynapse impl are all
unreferenced — remove them.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: remove stale RoomManagementApi references from CLAUDE.md

RoomManagementApi was removed in the space support refactor.
Reconciliation now uses MatrixService directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

  incremental build fixes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rework NB08 Section 2 pedagogy: fix flow, comments, and alpha labels

- undistort-methods markdown: add ASCII diagram showing the black-border
  problem BEFORE introducing getOptimalNewCameraMatrix so beginners

commit.message:
chore: migrate from npm to bun (#166)

* chore: migrate from npm/pnpm to bun

- Replace all npm/pnpm commands with bun in Makefile, CI workflows, and scripts
- Update devcontainer Dockerfile to install bun system-wide
- Update post-create.sh to use bun for dependency installation
- Add lefthook.yml for git hooks (replaces husky)
- Remove .husky directory and old pre-commit script
- Remove all package-lock.json files, add bun.lock
- Update documentation to show bun as recommended package manager
- Fix TypeScript error in sdk-react test-utils for bun compatibility

Bun provides faster package installation and runtime compared to npm/pnpm.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): make lefthook install conditional and remove duplicate bun setup

- Changed prepare script to check if lefthook exists before running
- Removed duplicate "Setup Bun" step in lint-typescript job that used
  "latest" version (kept the pinned 1.2.15 version)

This fixes CI failures where bun install would fail because lefthook
wasn't installed when the prepare script ran.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update bun.lock after rebasing on main

Updated lockfile to reflect dependency updates from main branch:
- @tanstack/react-router: ^1.166.4
- @tanstack/react-router-devtools: ^1.166.4
- @tanstack/router-plugin: ^1.166.4
- typescript-eslint: ^8.57.0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): use bunx instead of bun exec for tsc commands

bun exec interprets flags like -b as its own arguments, causing failures.
bunx (like npx) correctly passes all arguments to the binary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(dev): reduce memory pressure during compilation with GOGC=50

The Elasticsearch v8/v9 client libraries contain large amounts of
auto-generated code that can cause OOM kills in memory-constrained
environments like devcontainers. Setting GOGC=50 makes the garbage
collector more aggressive, reducing peak memory usage during
compilation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(docker): update bun to 1.3.10 for Vite 7 compatibility

- Upgrade bun from 1.2.15 to 1.3.10 (includes Node.js 22.14+)
- Add docs/package.json to workspace copy for bun install
- Exclude nested node_modules in .dockerignore
- Remove --frozen-lockfile to allow lockfile regeneration
- Use bun run --cwd for workspace builds

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix period filter to use invoiceDate instead of receivedDate

Switch from receivedDate (upload date) to invoiceDate (billing date) as
the primary period filter for dashboard stats and period summaries. This
ensures invoices appear under the correct billing month. Revenue/costs
are now calculated by invoiceDate in the period, not by bank transaction
date (Zuflussprinzip), which is more intuitive for the monthly overview.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: Read file_id directly from JSON instead of extracting from link

The bug was that parse_cleanup_report() extracted file_id from the link
using regex, instead of reading it directly from the JSON 'id' field.
This caused file deletion to fail silently.

Changes:
- Read 'id' field directly from JSON (both scripts use this)
- Fall back to link extraction for backwards compatibility
- Handle different size formats (size_formatted vs size bytes)

This fixes the issue where files were not moved to trash despite
user confirming deletion.

feat: implement five-stage workbook parsing pipeline with Data Quality page

- Add strict three-layer field model (Raw / Normalized / Derived) across all row types
- New workbookParser.ts: five-stage pipeline (Validation → Raw Extraction → Normalization → Classification → Assembly)
- New ignoreRules.ts: modular, data-driven ignore rules engine (MM1300/1410/1600, valuation keyword, zero-amount sentinel)
- New workbook.ts types: DataFoundationRawRow, CashflowNormalizedRow, ClassifiedCashflowRow, WorkbookSession, ParseSummary
- New DataQualityPage: parse summary, borrowings coverage, unmapped review table, ignored rows table (paginated)
- UploadPage: async parseWorkbook() pipeline, UploadSuccess screen with 4-metric summary + dual CTAs
- AppContext: workbookSession state alongside legacy parsedData for chart tabs
- excelParser.ts: generic legacy parser for existing PortfolioTab / ExpenseTrackingTab / RepaymentsTab (mock fallback)
- UpdateType code and UpdateType desc kept strictly separate throughout; raw values never rewritten
- UNMAPPED_REVIEW rows always surfaced explicitly; borrowingsRelevant is audit-only flag

commit.message:
ICT Sentinel v5.0 — H4/H1 OB loops, Discount/Premium/OTE, % equity risk

Core improvements:
- H4 + H1 OB scanning via f_scan_obs() loop (not just last bar)
  · Finds real ICT OBs: last candle before impulse move
  · Mitigation check: OBs already traded through are skipped
  · Same function called via request.security for both timeframes
- Discount/Premium filter: longs only below 50% of range, shorts above 50%
- OTE filter: entries only at 61.8-79% Fibonacci retracement (optimal entry)
- Risk changed to % of equity (compounds with account growth, 1% default)
- Judas Swing flag: identifies high-confidence first-session-hour sweeps
- Max 1 trade per day hardened, weekly/daily caps shown in info table
- Fib levels plotted on chart (EQ, OTE long/short zones)
- H4 OB boxes with thick border, H1 OB boxes lighter weight
